risk_if_not_implemented: Without Certificate Authorities, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Cryptographic Key Management
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate cryptographic key management controls to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of keys.
compensating_control_1.justification: Cryptographic Key Management (CRY-09) provides cryptographic protection that compensates for the absence of Certificate Authorities (CRY-11) by ensuring data confidentiality and integrity through alternative technical means. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to securely implement an internal Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) infrastructure or obtain PKI services from a reputable PKI service provider.
compensating_control_2.justification: Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) (CRY-08) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Certificate Authorities (CRY-11) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
